<!-- Thanks for the contribution! Please fill in this template. -->

## Summary

<!-- One paragraph: what changes, and why. -->

## Linked issue

<!-- Closes #N  /  Refs #N. If there is no linked issue and the change is non-trivial, consider opening one first. -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing behaviour to change)
- [ ] Documentation only
- [ ] Tooling / CI / build configuration

## How was this verified?

<!-- Concrete steps. "Ran make check-all" is fine if the change is small; for larger changes, describe the scenarios you exercised. -->

- [ ] `make check-all` passes locally
- [ ] New / updated tests cover the change
- [ ] Manual verification (describe what you ran):

## Checklist

- [ ] My code follows the conventions in `CONTRIBUTING.md` (ruff + mypy strict on src/, pre-commit clean).
- [ ] I have added a `# SOURCE:` citation for any non-trivial test value (per the project's anti-hallucination convention).
- [ ] If I added a dependency, I have updated `verified-deps.toml` with version + doc URL + methods used.
- [ ] If this changes user-visible behaviour, I have updated `README.md` and / or `CHANGELOG.md` accordingly.
- [ ] I have **not** committed any API keys, generated audio, or copyrighted comic content.
